justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Regional isolation model with EU/US/APAC as independent deployments with own databases, no raw PII crossing regions. (2) Data residency compliance addressed for both GDPR and CCPA explicitly. (3) Global dashboard uses aggregated metrics via Global Metrics Aggregator, never cross-region raw data. (4) Courier assignment regional isolation enforced at domain model level (Courier aggregate region-lock invariant). (5) Trade-offs explicitly include 3x infrastructure cost and operational overhead of 3 regional deployments. (6) Degraded EU service during outage designed with circuit breaker, cached read-only responses, no writes accepted.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough regional isolation model with enforcement at three levels: infrastructure, platform, and application
Global dashboard design is well-articulated — metrics-only global tier with no PII exposure, using Published Language pattern
Trade-offs are detailed and include rejected alternatives with clear reasoning
Risks section identifies critical compliance risks (PII leakage via logging, Geo-DNS misconfiguration) with concrete mitigations
Degraded mode design includes TTLs, staleness indicators, and explicit constraints (no writes, stale data acknowledgment)
Implementation guidance provides a phased, reversible rollout strategy
APAC data residency requirements acknowledged as low-confidence assumption — could have been more explicit about which specific regulations apply beyond GDPR/CCPA
Defense-in-depth approach to Geo-DNS with application-layer region validation is a strong addition beyond the rubric requirements

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using region-sovereign data planes for EU, US, and APAC with a lightweight global control plane for cross-region observability. Each region owns its customer and courier data end-to-end, enforcing data residency at the infrastructure level. A global metrics aggregation layer (not raw data) feeds the operations dashboard. Regional failover uses active-passive within each region's availability zones rather than cross-region failover, preser
